Name: Pattern Recognition - PE

Name: Backtracking - PE

Name: Validation - NE
Text: But precedence(EF) means E must precede F. So E must come before F. But EDG are consecutive. So F must come after G or before E. If F comes before E: A  B  C  F  H  E  D  G  H But then we need E to precede F, and F is before E. Violation!

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Let me try: A  B  C  H  E  D  G  H  F precedence(EF): E at 5, F at 9. E precedes F.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the noncoexistence constraint between G and A makes it impossible for both to occur in the same trace, but existence(A) and init(A) require A, and the chain constraints lead to G. This is indeed a contradiction in the model.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Actually, thinking about it more, perhaps the model is intentionally designed to demonstrate various DECLARE templates, and some constraints may be contradictory in practice. This is actually common in real process mining scenarios where discovered models may have conflicting constraints that need human interpretation.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
